mnemonics.title: Contract Types Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: FIRM: Fixed Price (High vendor risk), Time & Materials (High client risk), Cost Plus (Shared risk), Incentive (Shared reward & risk)

mnemonics.title: Contract Lifecycle Phases
mnemonics.content: NEMAR: Negotiation - Execution - Monitoring - Amendment - Renewal/Termination

mnemonics.title: Key Contract Elements
mnemonics.content: PARTS: Parties - Activities - Resources - Timeframe - Standards

mnemonics.title: SLA Metrics
mnemonics.content: DARTS: Downtime - Availability - Response time - Throughput - Success rate

acronyms.usage: Defines expected service standards like uptime (99.9%), response times, and penalties for non-compliance
Document soliciting detailed proposals from vendors including approach, cost, and timeline
Detailed description of project scope, deliverables, milestones, and acceptance criteria
Legal document ensuring confidentiality of shared business and technical information
Legal ownership rights over software code, documentation, and inventions
Contract type where client pays for actual time and resources expended
Hierarchical decomposition of project scope into manageable work packages

visualTips.concept: Fixed Price vs T&M Risk Distribution
visualTips.visualization: Draw a scale: Fixed Price - vendor side heavy (vendor bears cost risk); T&M - client side heavy (client pays overruns)
Draw a circular flow: Initiation → Negotiation → Signing → Execution → Monitoring → Amendment → Termination/Renewal
Traffic light system: Green (meets SLA), Yellow (at risk), Red (breached with penalties)
Flowchart: Change Request → Impact Assessment → Approval (Client) → Cost/Timeline Update → Contract Amendment

analogies.analogy: Like ordering a pizza for a fixed price - you know total cost, restaurant bears ingredients cost risk
Like hiring a plumber who charges hourly - you pay for actual time, risk of bill increasing
Like a warranty on electronics - promises specific performance, remedies if promises broken
Like a marriage: courtship (negotiation), wedding (signing), marriage (execution), possible counseling (amendment), divorce or renewal (termination/renewal)
Like insurance - each party covers what they're best equipped to handle, not who can afford it
